root_text.sn22.111:1.2: “Rūpe, bhikkhave, yo chando yo rāgo yā nandī yā taṇhā, taṁ pajahatha.
root_text.sn22.111:1.3: Evaṁ taṁ rūpaṁ pahīnaṁ bhavissati ucchinnamūlaṁ tālāvatthukataṁ anabhāvaṅkataṁ āyatiṁ anuppādadhammaṁ.
root_text.sn22.111:1.7: viññāṇe yo chando yo rāgo yā nandī yā taṇhā, taṁ pajahatha.
root_text.sn22.111:1.8: Evaṁ taṁ viññāṇaṁ pahīnaṁ bhavissati ucchinnamūlaṁ tālāvatthukataṁ anabhāvaṅkataṁ āyatiṁ anuppādadhamman”ti.

translation_text.sn22.111:1.2: “Mendicants, you should give up any desire, greed, relishing, and craving for form.
translation_text.sn22.111:1.3: Thus that form will be given up, cut off at the root, made like a palm stump, obliterated, and unable to arise in the future.
translation_text.sn22.111:1.4: You should give up any desire, greed, relishing, and craving for feeling …
translation_text.sn22.111:1.8: Thus that consciousness will be given up, cut off at the root, made like a palm stump, obliterated, and unable to arise in the future.”
